title: Greek Dressing
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no animal-derived ingredients. The vinegar, lemon juice, acids, seasonings, aquafaba, mustard, nutritional yeast, and olive oil contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the dressing is plant-based, uses extra-virgin olive oil as the fat source, and gets body from aquafaba and emulsion technique rather than dairy, saturated animal fat, or cholesterol-containing ingredients.
intro: Greek diner dressing is a sharp, oregano-heavy salad dressing with enough body to cling to cold lettuce, tomatoes, onions, cucumbers, beans, or grilled vegetables. The classic flavor lands somewhere between vinaigrette and creamy dressing: tangy, herbal, garlicky, and rounded without needing dairy. This version uses a careful acid blend, aquafaba, and a strong emulsion to create that familiar diner-style cling while keeping the dressing bright and clean.

stepGroups.title: Build the acid base
items: Combine 3 tablespoons red wine vinegar, 1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice, ⅛ teaspoon malic acid, ⅛ teaspoon lactic acid powder, 1 teaspoon dried oregano, ½ teaspoon garlic powder, ½ teaspoon onion powder, ½ teaspoon fine sea salt, ¼ teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon nutritional yeast, and ½ teaspoon Dijon mustard in a tall blending jar.
Whisk the mixture thoroughly and let it stand for 5 minutes so the dried oregano hydrates and the powdered seasonings dissolve into the acid base.

stepGroups.title: Emulsify the dressing
items: Add ¼ cup aquafaba and 1 tablespoon cold water to the acid base. Blend with an immersion blender for 10 seconds to aerate and loosen the mixture.
With the immersion blender running, slowly stream in 3 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil. Continue blending for 20 to 30 seconds, until the dressing turns opaque, creamy, and lightly thickened.

stepGroups.title: Chill and set
items: Transfer the dressing to a jar and refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving. The rest allows the oregano to soften, the acid to round out, and the emulsion to develop a diner-style cling.
Shake well before serving. Refrigerate for up to 5 days.
